diagrams.title: Master Theorem Comparison Diagram
diagrams.description: Visual comparison of three cases of Master Theorem showing relationship between recursive work n^log_b(a) and combining work f(n)
elements: Case 1: n^log_b(a) > f(n) - recursive part dominates
Case 3: n^log_b(a) < f(n) - combining work dominates
n^log_b(a) = work at root of recursion tree
diagrams.purpose: Understanding asymptotic analysis of divide-and-conquer algorithms

diagrams.title: Recursion Tree for T(n) = 2T(n/2) + n²
diagrams.description: Three-level recursion tree showing how work is distributed across levels when analyzing divide-and-conquer recurrence
elements: Level 2: 2 nodes each with (n/2)² = n²/4 work, total = n²/2
Level 3: 4 nodes each with (n/4)² = n²/16 work, total = n²/4
Sum of geometric series: n² + n²/2 + n²/4 + ... = 2n² = Θ(n²)
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how recursion tree method works for solving recurrences
